# New York city guide

## Overview

New York City is the city that actually never sleeps. Five boroughs, 800 languages spoken, and a pizza slice on every corner. It's loud, expensive, overwhelming, and completely addictive. Whatever you're into, NYC has the best version of it somewhere.

**Best time to visit**: Fall (Sep-Nov) for crisp weather and Central Park foliage, Spring (Apr-May) for cherry blossoms and outdoor dining.
**Currency**: US Dollar (USD).
**Language tip**: New Yorkers speak fast and walk faster. Be direct, don't block the sidewalk, and you'll fit right in.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lower Manhattan/SoHo | Trendy, artsy | Shopping, galleries, cobblestone streets | N/R/W to Prince St |
| Greenwich Village | Charming, bohemian | Jazz clubs, comedy, brownstone walks | A/C/E to W 4th St |
| Williamsburg (Brooklyn) | Hipster, foodie | Brunch, vintage, waterfront views | L train to Bedford Ave |
| Harlem | Cultural, historic | Soul food, jazz, Apollo Theater | 2/3 to 125th St |
| Chelsea | Art, upscale | High Line, galleries, Chelsea Market | C/E to 23rd St |
| Lower East Side | Gritty, trendy | Cocktail bars, live music, dumplings | F to Delancey St |
| Midtown | Iconic, touristy | Times Square, Broadway, Empire State Building | Multiple lines, Times Square |
| DUMBO (Brooklyn) | Scenic, photogenic | Brooklyn Bridge views, Grimaldi's, art | F to York St |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Joe's Pizza | Pizza | Greenwich Village | $ | The quintessential New York slice. Thin, foldable, perfect. Cash only, no-frills, exactly right. | Plain cheese slice |
| Katz's Delicatessen | Deli | Lower East Side | $$ | Pastrami on rye since 1888. "I'll have what she's having" was filmed here. The sandwiches are enormous. | Pastrami on rye |
| Los Tacos No. 1 | Mexican | Chelsea Market | $ | The best tacos in Manhattan. Fresh tortillas pressed to order, adobo chicken that drips perfection. | Adobo chicken taco, nopal taco |
| Peter Luger Steak House | Steakhouse | Williamsburg | $$$ | Cash only, no menu needed. The porterhouse has been the benchmark since 1887. | Porterhouse for two, creamed spinach |
| Xi'an Famous Foods | Chinese | Multiple | $ | Hand-pulled noodles and cumin lamb that launched a NYC obsession. Spicy, oily, magnificent. | Spicy cumin lamb noodles, liang pi |
| Levain Bakery | Bakery | Upper West Side | $ | Six-ounce cookies that are crispy outside, molten inside. The chocolate chip walnut is legendary. | Chocolate chip walnut cookie |
| Di Fara Pizza | Pizza | Midian (Brooklyn) | $$ | Dom DeMarco hand-cuts basil on each slice. Worth the trek to Brooklyn for the best Sicilian square. | Square slice, regular round |
| Russ and Daughters | Appetizing | Lower East Side | $$ | Smoked fish, bagels, and cream cheese since 1914. The classic bagel with lox is a NYC rite of passage. | Classic bagel with lox, whitefish salad |
| Momofuku Noodle Bar | Asian fusion | East Village | $$ | David Chang's original restaurant. The pork buns changed dining in NYC. | Pork belly buns, ramen |
| Juliana's Pizza | Pizza | DUMBO | $$ | Patsy Grimaldi's newer spot across from his old one. Coal-fired, blistered crust, perfect margherita. | Margherita pizza |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Central Park | Nature | Midtown/UWS/UES | Free | 2-4 hours | 843 acres of green in the middle of Manhattan. Bethesda Fountain, Bow Bridge, the Ramble. Go at sunrise. |
| Statue of Liberty and Ellis Island | History | Harbor | $$ | 4 hours | Book the pedestal or crown tickets months ahead. Ellis Island immigration museum is deeply moving. |
| Brooklyn Bridge Walk | Walk | DUMBO/Lower Manhattan | Free | 1 hour | Walk from Brooklyn to Manhattan for the best views. Go early morning to avoid crowds. |
| Metropolitan Museum of Art | Museum | Upper East Side | $$ | 3-4 hours | One of the world's greatest museums. The rooftop bar in summer has stunning Central Park views. |
| The High Line | Walk | Chelsea | Free | 1-2 hours | An elevated park built on old rail tracks. Public art, gardens, and city views. Walk south to north. |
| Empire State Building | Views | Midtown | $$$ | 1-2 hours | The classic NYC observation deck. Go at night for city lights. The 86th floor outdoor deck is the one. |
| 9/11 Memorial and Museum | History | Lower Manhattan | Free-$$ | 2-3 hours | The reflecting pools are powerful and quiet. The underground museum tells the full story. |
| Broadway Show | Entertainment | Midtown | $$$  | 3 hours | TKTS booth in Times Square sells same-day discounted tickets. Line up an hour before opening. |
| Top of the Rock | Views | Midtown | $$$ | 1 hour | Better views than Empire State because you can see the Empire State. Sunset timing is perfect. |
| Grand Central Terminal | Architecture | Midtown | Free | 30 min | The main concourse ceiling with its zodiac mural is breathtaking. The Whispering Gallery is a fun trick. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Please Don't Tell (PDT) | Speakeasy | East Village | $$$ | Enter through a phone booth inside a hot dog shop. Craft cocktails in a hidden basement. Reservations essential. |
| House of Yes | Club/performance | Bushwick | $-$$ | Part nightclub, part circus, part art performance. Themed nights, costumes encouraged. Brooklyn's wildest venue. |
| Blue Note | Jazz club | Greenwich Village | $$-$$$ | World-famous jazz venue. Late shows are cheaper. Intimate room, incredible sound. |
| Bemelmans Bar | Cocktail bar | Upper East Side | $$$ | Art Deco bar in the Carlyle Hotel with Madeline murals. Live piano, old-money elegance. |
| Rooftop at Pier 17 | Rooftop bar | Seaport | $$ | Open-air rooftop with Brooklyn Bridge views. Summer concerts and sunset cocktails. |

## Transit tips

- **MetroCard or OMNY**: Tap contactless cards or phones at OMNY readers. MetroCards still work at all stations.
- **Subway**: Runs 24/7 but late-night service is reduced. Express trains skip stops, check the map carefully.
- **Walking**: Manhattan is a grid. Avenues run north-south, streets run east-west. 20 blocks equals about 1 mile.
- **Cabs vs rideshare**: Yellow cabs are everywhere in Manhattan. Rideshare is often cheaper outside Midtown.
- **Airports**: JFK is far (1+ hours). Use AirTrain plus subway. LaGuardia is closer but has worse transit connections.
- **Pro tip**: Download the Citymapper app. It handles subway, bus, ferry, and walking directions perfectly.
- **Ferries**: NYC Ferry runs to Brooklyn, Queens, and the Bronx. Same price as the subway with better views.
- **Free ferry**: Staten Island Ferry is free and passes the Statue of Liberty. Great budget sightseeing.

## Useful local phrases

| English | NYC slang | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| Excuse me | Excuse me (said fast) | Pronounced "scuse me" while already pushing past |
| Hero/sub/grinder | A hero | A large sandwich on a long roll |
| Standing in line | On line | New Yorkers stand "on line" not "in line" |
| Houston Street | HOW-ston | Not like Houston, Texas |
| Regular coffee | Regular | Coffee with milk and sugar at a bodega |
| Dollar slice | Dollar slice | $1 pizza, available 24/7 in Midtown |
| Bridge and tunnel | B and T | Someone from outside Manhattan |
| Bodega | Bodega | Corner convenience store, often with a cat |
